# gatoRs: Geographic and Taxonomic Occurrence R-Based Scrubbing

**Natalie N. Patten, Michelle L. Gaynor, Douglas E. Soltis, and Pamela
S. Soltis**

## Overview

gatoRs (Geographic and Taxonomic Occurrence R-Based Scrubbing) provides
users with tools for downloading and processing biodiversity data. Click
[here](https://nataliepatten.github.io/gatoRs/articles/Introduction.html)
for more information.

## Installation

    install.packages("devtools")
    devtools::install_github("nataliepatten/gatoRs")

## Quick Start

Our package aims to streamline downloading and processing of
biodiversity specimen data. Here is a quick example of how to download
and clean with our package.

Step 1: Download

    library(gatoRs)
    galaxdf <- gators_download(synonyms.list = c("Galax urceolata", "Galax aphylla"),
                    write.file = FALSE,
                    gbif.match = "fuzzy",
                    idigbio.filter = TRUE)

Step 2: Clean  
- We do not recommend jumping to our full clean function. See our
extended introduction [here](https://nataliepatten.github.io/gatoRs/)!

    clean_data <- full_clean(galaxdf,
                             synonyms.list = c("Galax urceolata", "Galax aphylla"),
                             digits = 3,
                             basis.list = c("Preserved Specimen","Physical specimen"),
                             accepted.name = "Galax urceolata")

The gatoRs logo was created using the hexSticker package
(<https://github.com/GuangchuangYu/hexSticker>).

# Package index

## Download data

Wrapper functions to download data from GBIF and iDigBio.

- [`gators_download()`](https://nataliepatten.github.io/gatoRs/reference/gators_download.md)
  : Download - Download specimen data from both GBIF and the iDigBio
  archive

## Clean data

Various tools to clean downloaded data produced from our download
functions.

- [`needed_records()`](https://nataliepatten.github.io/gatoRs/reference/needed_records.md)
  : Identify Missing Information - Find records with redacted or missing
  data
- [`need_to_georeference()`](https://nataliepatten.github.io/gatoRs/reference/need_to_georeference.md)
  : Identify Missing Information - Find records which lack coordinate
  information
- [`remove_redacted()`](https://nataliepatten.github.io/gatoRs/reference/remove_redacted.md)
  : Remove Redacted Information - Remove protected or private records
  prior to publication
- [`remove_missing()`](https://nataliepatten.github.io/gatoRs/reference/remove_missing.md)
  : Remove Missing Information - Prepare to merge a data frame with
  georeferenced and retrieved records
- [`gators_merge()`](https://nataliepatten.github.io/gatoRs/reference/gators_merge.md)
  : Merge Retained Data - Combined original data set with georeferenced
  or retained records.
- [`remove_duplicates()`](https://nataliepatten.github.io/gatoRs/reference/remove_duplicates.md)
  : Remove Duplicates - Remove records with identical event dates and
  coordinates
- [`taxa_clean()`](https://nataliepatten.github.io/gatoRs/reference/taxa_clean.md)
  : Taxonomic Cleaning - Filter and resolve taxon names
- [`basis_clean()`](https://nataliepatten.github.io/gatoRs/reference/basis_clean.md)
  : Basis Cleaning - Removes records with certain record basis
- [`basic_locality_clean()`](https://nataliepatten.github.io/gatoRs/reference/basic_locality_clean.md)
  : Locality Cleaning - Remove missing and improbable coordinates
- [`process_flagged()`](https://nataliepatten.github.io/gatoRs/reference/process_flagged.md)
  : Locality Cleaning - Find possibly problematic occurrence records
- [`thin_points()`](https://nataliepatten.github.io/gatoRs/reference/thin_points.md)
  : Spatial Correction - Spatially thin records
- [`one_point_per_pixel()`](https://nataliepatten.github.io/gatoRs/reference/one_point_per_pixel.md)
  : Spatial Correction - One point per pixel
- [`full_clean()`](https://nataliepatten.github.io/gatoRs/reference/full_clean.md)
  : Full Cleaning - Wrapper function to speed clean
- [`data_chomp()`](https://nataliepatten.github.io/gatoRs/reference/data_chomp.md)
  : Subset Data - Get species, longitude, and latitude columns
- [`citation_bellow()`](https://nataliepatten.github.io/gatoRs/reference/citation_bellow.md)
  : Cite Data - Get GBIF citations

## Helper functions

These functions are used within the download and cleaning functions.

- [`get_gbif()`](https://nataliepatten.github.io/gatoRs/reference/get_gbif.md)
  : Used in gators_download() - Download data from the Global
  Biodiversity Information Facility
- [`setupduckDB()`](https://nataliepatten.github.io/gatoRs/reference/setupduckDB.md)
  : Used in gators_download() - Setting up local query
- [`get_idigbio_arc()`](https://nataliepatten.github.io/gatoRs/reference/get_idigbio_arc.md)
  : Used in gators_download() - Download data from Integrated Digitized
  Biocollections that are not on GBIF
- [`filter_fix_names()`](https://nataliepatten.github.io/gatoRs/reference/filter_fix_names.md)
  : Used in gators_download() - Filter iDigBio results by scientific
  name
- [`fix_columns()`](https://nataliepatten.github.io/gatoRs/reference/fix_columns.md)
  : Used in gators_download() - Fill out taxonomic name columns
- [`fix_names()`](https://nataliepatten.github.io/gatoRs/reference/fix_names.md)
  : Used in gators_download() - Fix taxonomic name capitalization
- [`remove_skewed()`](https://nataliepatten.github.io/gatoRs/reference/remove_skewed.md)
  : Used in basic_locality_clean() - Removed skewed locality
